/cwe/weakness/431.Name: Missing Handler
/cwe/weakness/431.Description: A handler is not available or implemented.
/cwe/weakness/431.ExtendedDescription: When an exception is thrown and not caught, the process has given up an opportunity to decide if a given failure or event is worth a change in execution.

Entries.IntroText: If a Servlet does not catch all exceptions, it may reveal debugging information that will help an adversary form a plan of attack. In the following method a DNS lookup failure will cause the Servlet to throw an exception.

Entries.BodyText: When a Servlet throws an exception, the default error response the Servlet container sends back to the user typically includes debugging information. This information is of great value to an attacker.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Handle all possible situations (e.g. error condition).
If an operation can throw an Exception, implement a handler for that specific exception.
